remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Spontaneous regression is rare (<0.5% of cases); 5-year survival without treatment: <5% for advanced NSCLC; ~20% for stage I-II with surgery
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: NSCLC with driver mutations (EGFR, ALK, ROS1, MET, KRAS G12C): targeted therapy achieves 70-90% response rates; PFS 16-19 months with osimertinib (FLAURA2 combination up to 25 months); immunotherapy (pembrolizumab): 5-year OS ~31% in high PD-L1 expressers vs ~16% chemotherapy; SBRT for early-stage inoperable: 80-90% local control at 3 years
remission.gap_size: Very large between targeted/immunotherapy eligible and chemotherapy-only; large between treated and screened populations
remission.barrier_detail: Biomarker testing (PD-L1, EGFR/ALK/ROS1 mutations) still not universal; only 5-6% of high-risk smokers undergo low-dose CT screening despite 20% mortality reduction (NLST trial); smoking cessation dramatically reduces incidence but is underfunded
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — osimertinib adjuvant ADAURA (Stage IB-IIIA EGFR+ NSCLC) standard of care; neoadjuvant chemo-immunotherapy (CheckMate 816) plus surgery now standard for resectable stage III; PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors first-line since 2016-2018; targeted therapy since 2010 (erlotinib)
remission.outdated_treatments: Empiric chemotherapy without biomarker testing (platinum-doublets as sole approach in biomarker-unknown patients); thoracic radiation without concurrent chemotherapy for locally advanced NSCLC; palliative care referral only at end-stage vs early concurrent palliative integration (NEJM 2010 landmark trial showing survival benefit with early palliative)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Liquid biopsy (ctDNA) for treatment monitoring and minimal residual disease detection; exercise prehabilitation pre-surgery (reduces postoperative complications 30-40%); pulmonary rehabilitation during treatment; mistletoe extract (Iscador) — Phase 3 data equivocal but widely used in European integrative oncology
remission.frontier_signal: KRAS G12C inhibitors (sotorasib, adagrasib — FDA approved 2021-2022); bispecific antibodies; TROP2-directed ADCs (datopotamab deruxtecan); TIGIT checkpoint combinations (Phase 3 ongoing); mRNA therapeutic vaccines for NSCLC (mRNA-4157 + pembrolizumab, KEYNOTE-942)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
